era_context: Early Bronze Age III; Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty 6) in its later phase; Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia; Indus Valley Civilization at maturity. This period represents the tail end of the Old Kingdom's centralized authority.

events.title: Pepi II's reign — Old Kingdom Egypt
events.description: Pepi II's reign continues. This year is part of the later phase of his reign, during which the Old Kingdom's administrative system was under increasing strain. Evidence suggests that the state continued to function, but with diminished central authority.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for this year.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for this year is minimal. No specific events are documented. The year is significant only as part of the broader context of Old Kingdom decline.
